title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The UK government has released a response to a report on artificial intelligence and public standards, outlining its plan to address the committee's recommendations. This plan focuses on ensuring AI is developed responsibly and benefits society.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This document assesses the impact of artificial intelligence systems on copyright law. It examines how copyright works are used in AI development and what implications this has on copyright protection.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education has a position on using generative AI to support education, and this document will outline their stance and guidelines for its application. This policy will likely address potential benefits, risks, and best practices for incorporating generative AI in educational settings.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport artificial intelligence action plan is a guide for working with the transport sector to take advantage of AI's benefits while minimizing its risks. It aims to promote collaboration between the Department for Transport and the transport industry to safely and effectively use artificial intelligence.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This progress report discusses the use of copyright works in developing artificial intelligence systems. It focuses on progress under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is responding to recommendations on how to regulate AI used in the medical field. The response outlines how the government plans to handle AI as a medical device.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's policy on using artificial intelligence in qualifications, as requested by government ministers to establish guidelines. It likely addresses how AI technology is used to assess and award qualifications in the UK.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government responds to the House of Lords Select Committee's report on artificial intelligence, outlining its plans to address the potential risks and opportunities of AI in the country. The response aims to implement recommendations to ensure the UK's AI development is beneficial and accountable.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a committee examining the intersection of artificial intelligence and public standards. The focus is on guiding the committee's review of AI's impact on public standards.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Centre for Science and Policy (CSPL) has submitted written evidence to an inquiry about how Artificial Intelligence (AI) is governed. The submission is aimed at informing the Science and Technology Committee's discussion on AI governance.
